title: Lattice Form Cell
description: A typed slot — an empty, sized, positioned box — produced by a Frame and occupied by a Tile or a nested Frame. The connective tissue of the Form composition model (design/forms.md §3, §5, §6). Cells are RESOLUTION-BLIND: geometry references CSS token names (e.g. --masthead-h, --frame-x) defined in lib/base, never px literals. See design/forms.md §6.

id.description: Cell identifier, kebab-case. Referenced by Tile.fits and Frame.cells/suppresses.

region.description: Band position on the slide (design/forms.md §5/§6). masthead carries the sub-cells masthead-lede (kicker+title) and masthead-bay (meta·logo·status); footer carries the three zones footer-left · progress-centre · pagination-right; overlay is the z4 annotation plane.

z.description: The Cell's content plane: 0 canvas · 1 atmosphere · 2 content · 3 chrome · 4 annotation (design/forms.md §5). For a `capacity: stack` Cell (e.g. stage), occupants may span lower planes — a surface Tile (z0/z1) stacks below the content Tile (z2) in the same Cell — so `z` is the Cell's principal plane, not a ceiling on every occupant.

accepts.description: Tile/Frame kinds that may dock in this Cell (the containment contract, design/forms.md §7). A Cell accepts by KIND, never by name (Open/Closed). 'frame' admits a nested Frame (the recursion).

capacity.description: Whether the Cell holds a single occupant ('one') or a z-stack of them ('stack').

fill.description: How an underfilling occupant distributes (design/forms.md §5). The difference between a 'grid of boxes' and a board deck.

gap.description: Guaranteed gap to neighbours, as a CSS token NAME (e.g. --frame-gap) defined in lib/base — referenced, never redefined here. See design/forms.md §6.

geometry.description: Resolution-blind geometry — references CSS token NAMES defined in lib/base/base.tokens.css / base.variants.css (e.g. --masthead-h, --frame-x). Never px literals (design/forms.md §6).

size.description: Token name(s) for the Cell's size (e.g. --masthead-h).

inset.description: Token name(s) for the Cell's inset from the frame edge (e.g. --frame-x).

clip.description: overflow:hidden — content is cut at the Cell, never bled across a neighbour (design/forms.md §6 clip guarantee).
